steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content:
Multiply the equation by $-1$ to get
$$3x^2+7x-1=0$$
Then use $a=3$, $b=7$, and $c=-1$:
$$x=\frac{-7\pm\sqrt{7^2-4(3)(-1)}}{2(3)}=\frac{-7\pm\sqrt{61}}{6}$$

steps.title: Choose the greater root
steps.content:
The greater solution uses the plus sign:
$$-\frac{7}{6}+\frac{\sqrt{61}}{6}$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the equation as written and compare the larger root to the answer choices. The answer is C.
